title: Crispy Onion Chicken with Mashed Potatoes & Green Beans
description: Home Chef customer favorite — chicken breasts coated in a crunchy French fried onion and panko crust, baked until golden and served with creamy mashed potatoes and tender green beans. The ultimate comfort dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Line a baking sheet with foil and spray with cooking spray.
Peel and cube potatoes. Boil in salted water for 15–18 minutes until fork-tender. Drain.
Crush French fried onions in a zip-lock bag or food processor until coarsely crumbled. Mix with panko in a shallow bowl.
Pat chicken dry. Brush each breast generously with Dijon mustard on both sides. Press firmly into the onion-panko mixture to coat.
Place coated chicken on the prepared baking sheet. Drizzle with olive oil. Bake 20–25 minutes until golden and cooked through (165°F).
Toss green beans with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread on a second baking sheet (or alongside chicken if there's room). Roast for the last 12–15 minutes of chicken cooking time.
Mash potatoes with butter, warm milk, minced garlic, salt, and pepper until smooth and creamy.
Serve crispy onion chicken over mashed potatoes with roasted green beans on the side.
tips: French fried onions (like French's brand) are the secret ingredient — they add incredible crunch and savory flavor.
Dijon mustard acts as the "glue" for the coating and adds a subtle tang.
For even crispier chicken, place on a wire rack over the baking sheet so air circulates underneath.
Leftover mashed potatoes can be made into potato cakes the next day.
